kinematicGroups._notes: Hand-verified throws (NOT the cam lobes at .156/.158/.160/.162). All 4 throws are parented to the crank_axis Empty in the live blend; crank_rotation animates the empty's X-axis rotation.

director_hints.scale: 750mm × 600mm × 510mm — full hero-sized assembly with deep internal anatomy
key_moves: the 8 pistons firing in sequence drive the crankshaft
director_hints.narration_tone: technical — there is deep internal anatomy worth explaining; 5-7 beats is fine.
do_not: do NOT use show_only on individual pistons — they are thin crowns that look like wireframe rings in isolation; prefer dim_others with the shell at 0.40-0.55
do NOT set scene.environment.background to a dark color — this asset now lives on cream paper
do NOT reference 'connecting rods' as a target — they are NOT modelled as separate meshes in this CAD

_curated: The 'hero_parts' map + 'aliases' are what the director should reference. Full 'parts' (673 entries) is retained only for runtime alias resolution.

internal_anatomy.rods_disabled_reason: This CAD does not model connecting rods as separate meshes; classifier 'connecting_rod' hits are spark-plug wires / hoses. Director should narrate around this (e.g. 'the connecting rod inside the block transmits the force...') without trying to highlight a rod mesh that doesn't exist.
internal_anatomy._guidance: Verified by hand. The director can address pistons by alias (piston_1A..piston_4B), crank throws by alias (crank_throw_1..4), and the connecting rods are NOT modeled in this CAD - narrate around them ('the rod inside the block transmits the force...') without trying to highlight a rod mesh.
